task.description: Review each analyzer output provided to you:
task.sub_task: Full_text analyzer: note all element IDs, types, reading order, and verbatim text. This is your TEXT source — every word must be preserved.
Elements analyzer: note hierarchical_level, section grouping, sub_type, and context for each element. This is your STRUCTURE source — it tells you how to nest.
Specialized analyzers (chart, diagram, table): note their element IDs, extracted data structures, and any figure references. These are your ENRICHMENT sources.

task.description: Using the elements analyzer's hierarchy data, determine the sect nesting:
task.sub_task: Identify all headings and their levels (H1-H6).
Determine which heading opens which section. H1 stays at root. Each H2 opens a depth-1 sect. Each H3 opens a depth-2 sect inside its H2 parent. Each H4 opens a depth-3 sect inside its H3 parent.
If the elements analyzer provides explicit section grouping or hierarchical_level, use it. If not, infer from heading levels.
Give each sect a descriptive id based on the heading text (e.g., sect_linear_equations, sect_methodology).
If a heading level is skipped in the source document (e.g., H1 → H3), note this and use the elements analyzer's guidance to assign the correct level.

task.description: Walk through the full_text elements in reading order and place each one into the correct sect:
task.sub_task: For each non-heading element, determine which sect it belongs in — it goes in the innermost currently-open sect at the point where it appears in reading order.
Preserve the EXACT element ID and verbatim text from the full_text analyzer.
Map the full_text element type to the correct PDF/UA tag: h1→H1, h2→H2, paragraph→P, figure→Figure, footnote→Note, header→Artifact, footer→Artifact, etc.
Figures MUST have an alt_text tag. Use the full_text analyzer's alt_text if available. Otherwise use the specialized analyzer's description.
Captions should include parent_ref pointing to their Figure or Table.

task.description: For each paragraph element, check if it contains inline semantic content:
task.sub_task: Mathematical formulas: look for equations (y = mx + b), variable notation (f(x)), Greek letters, subscripts. Create inline Formula elements.
Footnote references: look for superscript numbers (¹, ²) or symbols (*, †). Create inline Reference elements with ref pointing to the corresponding Note.
Hyperlinks: look for URLs or "click here" style text. Create inline Link elements.
Styled spans: if the elements analyzer identifies specific styled text within a paragraph (e.g., institution name, emphasis), create inline Span elements.
The parent element's text tag still contains the FULL verbatim text. The inline children specify which fragments are semantically distinct — they do not replace the text.

task.description: When a table is present (identified by full_text and/or table analyzer):
task.sub_task: Create a Table element with a children block.
If a caption exists, include it as the first child (Caption element).
Create THead with TR containing TH elements. Each TH MUST have scope="Column".
Create TBody with TR elements. If a row has a header cell, use TH with scope="Row". Data cells are TD.
Each TH and TD should contain a text tag with the cell's verbatim content.
If the table analyzer provides structured data, attach it as an enrichment inside the Table element.
Create an L element with a children block.
Each list item becomes LI containing Lbl (bullet/number) and LBody.
LBody contains one or more P elements with the item's text.
For nested lists (sub-items), place a nested L element inside the parent LBody.
Use the full_text analyzer's list content for text. If the elements analyzer identifies list structure, use it for nesting depth.

task.description: For each specialized analyzer output, attach its data to the correct element:
task.sub_task: Chart data → enrichments block inside the Figure element it corresponds to.
Diagram structure → enrichments block inside the Figure element.
Table structure → enrichments block inside the Table element.
Include the FULL structure from the specialized analyzer. Do not summarize or truncate.
Does text explicitly reference a figure? (e.g., "see Figure 3", "as shown in Table 1") → cross_reference with type="figure_callout" and evidence quoting the callout.
Does text describe content that a figure illustrates? → cross_reference with type="illustrated_by_figure" and evidence explaining the relationship.
Does a footnote reference pair with a note? → Reference element with ref pointing to Note, and Note with ref pointing back to Reference.
Every relationship MUST have explicit evidence. Do not fabricate relationships.
Build grounded_to_text blocks inside figure/table elements listing which text elements reference or are illustrated by them.

task.description: Handle elements that require special treatment:
task.sub_task: BlockQuotes: wrap in a sect with tag="BlockQuote". Content inside becomes P elements.
Bibliography entries: tag as BibEntry. Place inside a sect headed by the References heading.
Table of Contents: wrap in TOC, with each entry as TOCI containing inline Link elements.
Page numbers, running headers/footers: tag as Artifact. Place inside sect_root.
Block-level formulas (displayed equations): tag as Formula with placement="Block".
Root element: unified_document with session_id, page number, and schema_version="2.0".
Metadata: source_image path, correlation_timestamp, analyzers_executed with S3 URIs.
Content_tree: the hierarchical structure you've built.
Cross_reference_index: grouped by type (by_chart, by_table, by_diagram, by_note).
Raw_outputs: S3 URIs for all source analyzer results.
Summary: 2-4 sentences describing what was correlated, element count, tree depth, key relationships.
Every full_text element ID appears in the content_tree (no elements lost).
Every text tag contains exact verbatim text (no truncation).
Heading hierarchy has no level skips within any sect.
Every Figure has alt_text.
Every TH has a scope attribute.
Every Reference has a ref pointing to a Note, and vice versa.
Every Caption has a parent_ref pointing to its Figure or Table.
No enrichments were summarized or truncated.
schema_version="2.0" is present on the root element.
